title: astragalus astragatus
intro: Astragalus astragatus, also known as milk vetch or locoweed, is a perennial plant belonging to the legume family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory effects. The health benefits of Astragalus astragatus include its ability to enhance immune function, reduce stress, and improve cardiovascular health, as well as its potential to treat conditions such as diabetes, hypertension, and respiratory infections. Astragalus astragatus is typically prepared as a herbal tea, capsule, or tincture, and can also be consumed as a supplement in powder or extract form.
